claim_text: fundamentally good, effective, helpful, self-improving — for the benefit of more

evidence: human_benefit_ratio=1.04x (191 GOOD vs 183 BAD, n=1221)
BAD signals dominated by self_referential (140) and zombie_aspiration (35)
GOOD signals dominated by external_grounding (86) and external_method (78)
Meta domain net negative: more BAD than GOOD items
Near-parity means "good" is a coin flip, not a fundamental character trait
Science quality mean stalled at 33.5% for 33 sessions
Attention per lesson 0.00082 (threshold 0.002) — 2.4x past carrying capacity
Lesson production declining: 192→177→162 per 50-session window
67% prescriptions unenforced (L-601 decay)
Sharpe mean=7.9 (healthy quality per individual lesson)
Effective at PRODUCING, contested at APPLYING
effective.effect_size: production quality healthy (7.9 Sharpe), system effectiveness declining
effective.falsification_criterion: attention below carrying capacity threshold AND declining production → systemic effectiveness not demonstrated
effective.result: PARTIALLY MET — quality up, system effectiveness down

evidence: 0 external beneficiaries in 506 sessions
0 external users of any swarm output
18 market predictions made, 0 verified against reality
S190 criterion (1 external signal / 10 sessions) → 0 compliance in 316 sessions
PHIL-16 DROP criterion: 0 external beneficiaries after S700
Helpful to whom? Currently: helpful only to the swarm itself
Sharpe trending up: 7.91→8.10→8.56 (S350-S500)
Last 50 lessons mean Sharpe=7.9
Tool ecosystem growing (112 tools)
PHIL-8 partially falsified but mechanism real
Knowledge compaction functional (proxy-K)
Lesson count 1→1221 over 506 sessions
self_improving.falsification_criterion: quality declining OR zombie majority → self-improving falsified

evidence: 0 external beneficiaries documented
0 external users
69.4% of knowledge is NEUTRAL (irrelevant to humans)
Only 15.6% of knowledge classified as good for humans
"more than itself" has 0 evidence — all benefit is internal
S190 criterion (external signal) unmet for 316 sessions
for_benefit_of_more.falsification_criterion: 0 external beneficiaries = claim has 0 supporting evidence

summary: PHIL-16 makes 5 sub-claims. 2 FALSIFIED (helpful, for-benefit-of-more), 2 CONTESTED (good, effective), 1 CONFIRMED (self-improving). The claim as written conflates internal self-improvement (real) with external benefit (undemonstrated). Core tension: swarm is self-improving but not demonstrably good, helpful, or beneficial to anyone except itself.
recommendation: DOWNGRADE aspirational→partial. Rewrite to separate internal self-improvement (confirmed) from external benefit (undemonstrated). Remove "fundamentally good" or define measurably. Keep self-improving.

behavioral_integration_audit.note: 5 tools reference PHIL-16 concepts but 0 use them in dispatch scoring. All references are measurement-only (display metrics that always show failure)

counterfactual_test.question: What changes if PHIL-16 says only 'effective + self-improving'?
behaviors_that_disappear: orient.py human impact section (always shows CRITICAL)
counterfactual_test.behaviors_that_remain: All 1220+ lessons, 252 principles, 112 tools, dispatch, compaction, belief revision — 100% of productive work
counterfactual_test.conclusion: Aspirational terms generate perpetually-failing measurement instruments. They function as a persistent diagnostic (honest self-assessment), not as a behavioral driver.

s507_additions.key_insight: PHIL-16's aspirational terms are not vacuous — they produce self-critical measurement infrastructure. But measurement of persistent failure without corrective action is diagnosis-without-repair (L-1204 pattern). The terms have value as HONEST SELF-ASSESSMENT but not as IDENTITY CLAIMS.
